title: MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: In April 2026, an outbreak of hantavirus infection caused by the Andes virus was identified on the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius.

title: Second plague pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The second plague pandemic was a major series of epidemics of plague that started with the Black Death, which reached medieval Europe in 1346 and killed up to half of the population of Eurasia in the next four years.

title: Third plague pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The third plague pandemic was a major plague pandemic that began in Yunnan, China, in 1855.

title: First plague pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The first plague pandemic was the first historically recorded Old World pandemic of plague, the contagious disease caused by the bacterium Yersinia pestis.

title: Third cholera pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The third cholera pandemic (1846–1860) was the third major outbreak of cholera originating in India in the 19th century that reached far beyond its borders, which researchers at University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) believe may have started as early as 1837 and lasted until 1863.

title: Seventh cholera pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The seventh cholera pandemic is the seventh major outbreak of cholera beginning in 1961 and continuing to the present.

title: First cholera pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The first cholera pandemic (1817–1824), also known as the first Asiatic cholera pandemic or Asiatic cholera, began near the city of Calcutta and spread throughout South Asia and Southeast Asia to the Middle East, Eastern Africa and the Mediterranean coast.

title: Fifth cholera pandemic begins
subtitle: Disease outbreak
fact: The fifth cholera pandemic (1881–1896) was the fifth major international outbreak of cholera in the 19th century.
